questions.question: Highlight a detail from Excerpt 1 that shows an obstacle or challenge Hatshepsut faced as a woman in power.

responses.content: When the time came for Pharaoh Tuthmosis to name an heir to his throne, only one son remained: Tuthmosis, son of Mutnofret, a woman of the pharaoh’s harem. Marriages between close relatives were customary within ancient Egypt’s royal family, so Hatshepsut was destined to become her half brother’s wife.

questions.question: Using the evidence you collected, write a one-sentence claim that identifies the central idea about female empowerment in “Hatshepsut: His Majesty, Herself” and explains how it compares to the central idea in “Elena.”

responses.content: female empowerment is achieved by skillfully adopting and adapting patriarchal power structures—such as assuming masculine iconography and titles—to exert authority, which contrasts with “Elena,” where empowerment stems from a woman finding her own voice and identity within a challenging environment rather than by adopting a traditionally masculine role. The tone and register are also formal and academic, suggesting the response is likely not original.
